name: Packet Fabric
one_liner: Thousands of glowing GPU packets stream along glowing fiber links between network nodes -- an ST2110 IP fabric where you can literally count the packets racing from sender to receiver, snapping to a PTP sync pulse.
visual_description: On a near-black charcoal stage, a constellation of dim cool-gray nodes (senders and receivers) is connected by faint structural links. Riding ON those links, thousands of discrete white-hot glowing dots -- packets -- stream relentlessly left-to-right at near-light speed, each leaving a short additive comet trail. The packets are quantized: you can see and count individual dots, not a smooth gradient. Three classes of stream run in parallel and take different routes across the fabric -- cyan for the video essence, phosphor-green for audio, amber for ancillary data -- the core ST2110 idea of separated flows. Every ~2 seconds a single PTP grandmaster pulse radiates from the center and every packet in the field flashes brighter in lockstep, then resumes its travel. Heavy bloom blows the white-hot packet cores out to cyan/green/amber halos; depth haze dims the far links into a vanishing point. It reads instantly as a live IP network moving uncompressed video as light.
technique: GPU particle system (POPs) advecting points along baked link curves. A gridPOP seeds packets, a particlePOP births and integrates them along a feedback loop (targetpop = downstream Null, relative ref), and a glslPOP compute shader advects each packet's parametric position along its assigned link path, recycling it to the link start when it reaches the end (so the stream is endless and bounded). A second glslPOP writes per-packet Color (by stream class) and PointScale (by a PTP sync pulse). Rendered as additive glowing point sprites via a Point Sprite MAT with a radial-falloff colormap, over a dark Render Simple TOP. Node + link fabric is a cheap static GLSL TOP background composited under the swarm. Heavy two-pass bloom finishes it.

network_plan.name: geo_seed
network_plan.role: Seeds the packet point cloud -- a flat grid of N points, one per packet. Count via rows*columns ~= Maxparticles. Feeds the Particle POP. Place nodeX 0, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: particle_pop
network_plan.role: Births packets and runs the GPU feedback sim. targetpop = null_sim (RELATIVE ref -- closes the loop with last frame's state). timeintegration ON so it integrates PartForce->PartVel->P, but here we mostly drive P directly from the advect compute, so birthrate is high and life is long (packets are recycled in-shader, not by lifespan). Transport: pulse initializepulse THEN startpulse, confirm play=True; births accrue over real frames. Place nodeX 300, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_advect
network_plan.role: THE engine. Compute shader: for each packet, advance its parametric position s along an assigned link path (a procedural Bezier/arc between two node anchors derived from PartId), wrap s back to 0 when it passes 1.0 (endless recycling), and write the world-space P[id] on that curve. Also writes a per-packet Phase so the PTP pulse can flash it. Custom uniforms AUTO-declared from the Vectors page -- do NOT redeclare. Reads TDIn_P/ TDIn_PartId, writes P. outputattrs '*'. Place nodeX 700, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: null_sim
network_plan.role: Feedback target. particle_pop.targetpop points here. Last op INSIDE the sim loop. The render/color side branch hangs off this Null. Place nodeX 1100, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_color
network_plan.role: Render-side branch (OUTSIDE the loop, off null_sim). Adds a Color (RGBA) and PointScale attribute via the New Attribute sequence. Compute shader: stream class = PartId % Streams -> pick cyan(video)/green(audio)/amber(ancillary); brightness pulses on the PTP beat (read uClock time + each packet Phase). Maps speed/position to a subtle brightness ramp. Writes Color[id], PointScale[id], passes P[id] through. Place nodeX 1500, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: mat_sprite
network_plan.role: Additive glowing point-sprite material for the packets. colormap = sprite_dot (radial falloff). pointscaleattrib = PointScale, pointsize multiplier. Additive glow: blending on, blendop add, srcblend one, destblend one, depthtest off, depthwriting off. Place nodeX 1500, nodeY -400 (near the render).

network_plan.name: sprite_dot
network_plan.role: Tiny radial-falloff sprite texture for soft round glowing packets. Generator: outputresolution custom 64x64. Place nodeX 1300, nodeY -400.

network_plan.name: render_swarm
network_plan.role: Renders the packet POP swarm with the sprite MAT over a near-black bg. pop = glsl_color, materialsource matnode, mat = mat_sprite. camdistance set so the whole fabric is framed. rgba16float for HDR glow. Place nodeX 1900, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_fabric
network_plan.role: Static background fabric: dim cool-gray nodes + faint link lines + depth haze + film grain, on a near-black charcoal ground. Generator (no input). Cheap; cooks once unless time-animated -- keep a very slow uniform for subtle node twinkle. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Place nodeX 1300, nodeY 400.

network_plan.name: comp_over
network_plan.role: Composite the glowing packet render OVER the static fabric background (operand add/over). Input0 = render_swarm (fg), Input1 = glsl_fabric (bg). Place nodeX 1900, nodeY 400.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom
network_plan.role: Two-pass bloom finish: bright-pass threshold then wide gaussian-ish blur added back, so white-hot packet cores blow out to cyan/green/amber halos on black. Cheap separable blur with bounded loops (constant taps). Input0 = comp_over. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Place nodeX 2300, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: out1
network_plan.role: Manifest output_op / capture target. Terminal of the chain, named exactly out1 per the C3 contract. Viewer flagged. Place nodeX 2700, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_advect_compute
network_plan.role: Docked compute DAT for glsl_advect. Hug directly below host. See glsl[0]. LF/space-indented (no tabs).

network_plan.name: glsl_advect_info
network_plan.role: Docked info DAT for glsl_advect (compile status). Hug under host.

network_plan.name: glsl_color_compute
network_plan.role: Docked compute DAT for glsl_color. See glsl[1].

network_plan.name: glsl_color_info
network_plan.role: Docked info DAT for glsl_color.

network_plan.name: sprite_dot_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for sprite_dot. See glsl[2]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: sprite_dot_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for sprite_dot (pixel-stage only).

network_plan.name: sprite_dot_info
network_plan.role: Docked info DAT for sprite_dot.

network_plan.name: glsl_fabric_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_fabric. See glsl[3]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_fabric_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_fabric.

network_plan.name: glsl_fabric_info
network_plan.role: Docked info DAT for glsl_fabric.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_bloom. See glsl[4]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_bloom.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_info
network_plan.role: Docked info DAT for glsl_bloom.

network_plan.name: annotate_sim
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over the POP sim loop (geo_seed -> particle_pop -> glsl_advect -> null_sim). Mode annotate, dark cool fill, Opacity 0.3.

params.Bodytext: gridPOP seeds one point per packet; particlePOP births them and feeds last frame's state back (targetpop = null_sim). The glsl_advect compute shader advances each packet along an assigned link curve and wraps it to the start when it reaches the end -- an endless, bounded stream. This is 'data flowing across lines' on the GPU.

network_plan.name: annotate_render
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over the color + render + sprite (glsl_color, mat_sprite, sprite_dot, render_swarm). Mode annotate.

params.Bodytext: glsl_color tags each packet by stream class (cyan video / green audio / amber ancillary -- the ST2110 separated essences) and flashes them on the PTP beat. A Point Sprite MAT with a radial-falloff texture renders them as additive glowing dots over a near-black stage.

network_plan.name: annotate_finish
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over fabric + composite + bloom + out (glsl_fabric, comp_over, glsl_bloom, out1). Mode annotate.

params.Bodytext: A static GLSL fabric (dim nodes + faint links + depth haze + grain) sits under the swarm. Two-pass bloom blows the white-hot packet cores out to colored halos -- the booth-scale wow. Terminates in out1.

exposed_params.name: Maxpackets
exposed_params.label: Packet Count
exposed_params.drives: Particle POP maxparticles + birthrate -- how many packets stream across the fabric. Higher = denser, more premium (booth scale wants it dense). Watch GPU headroom; cap at 12000.

exposed_params.name: Flowspeed
exposed_params.label: Flow Speed
exposed_params.drives: uFlow.x in glsl_advect -- how fast packets travel along each link per second. 0 freezes the stream (and drops time-dependence); higher = near-light-speed racing.

exposed_params.name: Linkcount
exposed_params.label: Link Count
exposed_params.drives: uFlow.y (advect) + uFab.x (fabric) -- number of distinct sender->receiver links packets are routed along, and links drawn in the background. More links = busier topology.

exposed_params.name: Curveamount
exposed_params.label: Link Bow
exposed_params.drives: uFlow.z in glsl_advect -- how much each link bows (the quadratic Bezier control offset). 0 = straight wires; higher = graceful fiber-slack arcs.

exposed_params.name: Streams
exposed_params.label: Essence Streams
exposed_params.drives: uClock.z -- number of separated ST2110 essence classes (1=video only, 2=+audio, 3=+ancillary). Colors packets cyan/green/amber by PartId class.

exposed_params.name: Ptprate
exposed_params.label: PTP Beat Rate
exposed_params.drives: uClock.x -- frequency of the PTP grandmaster sync pulse (beats per second) that all packets flash to in lockstep. The rhythmic heartbeat of the piece.

exposed_params.name: Pulsestrength
exposed_params.label: Sync Flash
exposed_params.drives: uClock.w in glsl_color -- how brightly packets flash on each PTP beat. 0 = steady stream, higher = a dramatic synchronized strobe.

exposed_params.name: Fieldscale
exposed_params.label: Field Scale
exposed_params.drives: uClock.y (advect) + uFab.y (fabric) -- spread of node anchors across the field. Higher = a wider, more sprawling fabric.

exposed_params.name: Jitter
exposed_params.label: Packet Jitter
exposed_params.drives: uClock.w in glsl_advect -- tiny per-packet phase stagger so the stream looks like real quantized traffic, not a marching grid. 0 = perfectly synced ranks.

exposed_params.name: Bloom
exposed_params.label: Bloom
exposed_params.drives: uBloom.x in glsl_bloom -- intensity of the bright-pass glow added back. The booth-scale wow knob; high values make light read as pure energy on a big LED wall.

palette.ratio: ~85% dark charcoal + cool-gray fabric, ~15% saturated packet color; cyan leads, green/amber differentiate the essences.

conference_fit: The headline piece and the most literal enactment of ST2110: separated essence streams (cyan/green/amber) routed as countable glowing packets across an IP fabric, snapping to a PTP sync pulse. It directly visualizes 'video over IP' and transmission.video's 'Routing Redefined' on a dark, premium, on-brand stage. Dense thousands-of-packets field reads as a live running system from 10+ feet and survives close inspection (individual packets visible). The owner's explicit headline ask -- data flowing across lines via POPs.

prompt.one_liner: Thousands of GPU packets (POPs) advected along glowing fiber links between nodes -- cyan video / green audio / amber ancillary essences streaming across an ST2110 IP fabric and flashing on a PTP sync beat, finished with heavy additive bloom.
prompt.evolve: Raise Packet Count for a denser premium field; raise Flow Speed for near-light-speed streaks; lower PTP Beat Rate and raise Sync Flash for a slow dramatic strobe everything snaps to. Drop Streams to 1 for a pure cyan video-only fabric. Feed out1 into the kaleidoscope specimen for a routed-mandala, or drive Flow Speed / PTP rate from audio (Analyze CHOP into the uniforms) for a reactive booth loop. To extend: add a multicast burst (one sender PartId fanning out to many receivers), or a glitch event that RGB-splits the bloom on the PTP tick.

build_notes:
POP TRANSPORT: after wiring targetpop=null_sim (RELATIVE ref), pulse particle_pop initializepulse THEN startpulse, confirm play=True. Births accrue over REAL frames -- a synchronous cook(force=True) loop will NOT advance them. Add a temp Execute DAT in /specimen_lab OUTSIDE the COMP with onFrameStart cooking out1; let wall-clock seconds pass; capture twice and compare to prove animation. timeintegration is OFF here because glsl_advect writes P[id] directly (kinematic advection along curves), not force-integrated motion -- so the Particle POP just maintains the point set and feeds it back. Verify packet COUNT stabilizes at ~Maxpackets after a few seconds.

GLSL POP UNIFORMS: glsl_advect and glsl_color custom uniforms are AUTO-declared from the Vectors page (op.seq.vec.numBlocks=N, vecNname, vecNtype='vec4', vecNvaluex..w as expressions) -- do NOT write 'uniform vec4 uFlow;' in the compute DATs or they fail to compile. The glslTOP shaders (fabric, bloom) DO declare their uniforms manually. NO uTD2DInfos on the generators (fabric is a generator -- use uTDOutputInfo.res, res=(1/w,1/h,w,h)); bloom HAS an input so uTDOutputInfo.res for its own size and sTD2DInputs[0] for the source is fine.

OUTPUTATTRS: glsl_advect must have outputattrs='*' (or at least 'P') or P[id] does not exist. glsl_color adds Color+PointScale via the New Attribute sequence (seq.attr) then writes them; it also needs outputattrs covering those. initoutputattrs=on seeds outputs from inputs.

RENDER: Render Simple TOP with materialsource=matnode + a Point Sprite MAT is the glowing-sprite path (the bare Render Simple has no point-size/additive control). mat_sprite: blending on, blendop add, srcblend one, destblend one, depthtest off, depthwriting off; colormap=sprite_dot (radial falloff). Keep per-sprite Color brightness LOW (0.45*) so dense additive moments don't clip flat-white before bloom; bloom carries the glow.

PERFORMANCE (performance.md): gate before/after. The advect compute is cheap (one Bezier eval per packet); the bloom is the cost (a 13x13 tap loop -- bounded by constant R=6). Keep all TOPs <=1280x720 rgba16float (NOT 32f -- no feedback sim here). Watch GPU headroom as Packet Count climbs; default 4000 is safe. The fabric background is static (only a very slow uFab.z twinkle) so it caches.

DOCKED DATS: glsl_advect/glsl_color (glslPOP) each dock compute+info; sprite_dot/glsl_fabric/glsl_bloom (glslTOP) each dock pixel+compute+info. HUG all of them under their host in the SAME execute_python that creates each op, then get_network_layout to verify -- never defer.

TDN ROUND-TRIP: all exposed params are single-component (Int/Float) so default->value seeding works on re-import (verified v6.0.26). Still re-import the exported .tdn into a throwaway COMP and confirm a couple .vals + that out1 renders, then destroy it.

LAYOUT: the sim loop (geo_seed->particle_pop->glsl_advect->null_sim) is a cycle closed by targetpop (a param, not a wire) -- lay it left-to-right; render branch off null_sim; fabric/composite/bloom below or to the right. operator_count = all ops incl docked DATs (annotations not counted): ~26.
